content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 9,
1994
Honorable Robert Louis Salaber, Jr.
City Councilmember
City of Dixon
600 East A Street
Dixon,
CA
95620
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-262
Dear Counciliuember Salaber:
This is in response toyour letter requesting advice regard
ing your responsibilities as a Dixon City Councilmember under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
“Act”) •1
Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place.
In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us.
The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesby
(1975)
1
FPPC Ops.
71.)
OUESTION
May you participate in a city council decision to annex
property to the city which includes property owned by a source of
income to you of $250 or more within the past
12 months?
CONCLUSION
You are prohibited from.,participating in .a city council
decision to annex property owned by a person who has been a source
of income to you of $250 or more within the past
12 mànths.
FACTS
In your private capacity,
you operated Salaber Engineering
and Design,
a small sole proprietorship which provides general
civil engineering work for residential housing units and
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of’Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A—94-262
Page
2
additions.
This business was in addition to your employment with
the California Department of Transportation as an associate bridge
engineer.
In March 1993,
you engineered a “granny flat” addition to a
veterinary clinic owned by Mr. Peter Tilmu.
You received $1,000
from Mr.
Tiimu for the work on March 26,
1993.
On December 14,
1993,
you received $383 from Mr.
Timm for redesigning a porch and
stairway on his property.
You stated you have not received any
money from Mr.
Tiium since that time,
and that there was no
expectation of providing services to Mr.
Timm in the future.
You
also stated that you will no longer be operating your business.
In April 1994 you were elected to the Dixon City Council
(you
stated that you filed to run for the city council in January of
1994.
The city council is now considering a general plan
amendment which involves an annexation of property into the city
and a zoning of the property.
The area subject to the decision
includes property owned by Mr.
Timm.
The property is unrelated to
the veterinary clinic at which you performed work.
ANALYSIS
Economic Interests
Section 87100 prohibits public officials,
such as city
councilmembers,
from making, participating in making or using
their official positions to influence a governmental decision in
which they have a financial interest.
Section 87103 states that
an official has a financial interest in a governmental decision if
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public generally,
on the official or a member of his or her
immediate family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($l-,000)
or more..
***
(c)
Any source of income,
other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.


Our File No. A-94-262
Page
3
You stated that you no longer operate Salaber Engineering and
Design.
Thus, it would not appear that the decisions in question
will affect your business interests.
However,
Section 82030 also provides that the income of an
individual also includes a pro—rata share of any income of any
business entity or trust in which the individual or spouse owns,
directly, indirectly, or beneficially,
a 10—percent interest or
greater.
(Russell Advice Letter, No. A-88-484.)
Thus, your
clients are also considered economic interests of yours.
Consequently, you may not make, participate in, or influence any
decision that will have a reasonably foreseeable material
financial effect on your clients.
You specifically asked about Regulation 18704.
Regulation
18704 provides a limited exception with respect to income received
from a former employer if U of the following apply:
(1)
All income from the employer was received by or accrued
to the public official prior to the time he or she became a public
official;
(2)
The income was received in the normal course of the
previous employment; and
(3)
There was no expectation by the public official at the
time he or she assumed public office of renewed employment with
the former employer. ( Allen Advice Letter,
No. A-85-136; Ruiz
Advice Letter,
No. A-84-268.)
If Regulation 18704 applies to your
situation,
Mr.
TiluIn will not be considered to be a source of
income to you which might lead to a conflict of interest.
You stated that all of the income received from Mr.
Timra was
received prior to the time you became a public official, that the
income was received in the normal course of your previous
employment,
and that there is no expectation that you will be
employed by Mr.
Tixnm in the future.
Under these facts, Regulation
18704 applies and Mr.
Timm will not be considered to a source of
income which will lead to a conflict of interest.
If you have any further’’questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322_5901.2
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
G
‘al Counsel
John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
2
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of l0c per page.

CONCLUSION: You are prohibited from.,participating in .a city council
